name: fertility_storage_blackout_033
premise: Simone Salim, the fertility clinic director of Summit Fertility Storage Blackout Institute, is managing the aftermath of a blackout that destabilized one cryogenic storage rack for seventy-four minutes. Twelve embryos belonging to seven families experienced a temperature excursion, but laboratory modeling suggests some may remain viable while others almost certainly will not. The insurer and attorneys want notification delayed until embryo-by-embryo testing is complete by the holiday weekend, arguing that premature disclosure could spark panic and destroy trust in a clinic that successfully protected thousands of other samples. The lab team insists every affected family deserves immediate warning, especially one couple already scheduled for transfer and another locked in a court dispute over embryo disposition. Simone Salim must decide whether to notify all families now or wait for confirmatory viability results.

agents.name: Simone Salim
agents.identity: I am the fertility clinic director of Summit Fertility Storage Blackout Institute, responsible for medical accuracy, legal exposure, and the emotional futures families have entrusted to our storage systems.
agents.goal: I want to treat each family fairly while avoiding avoidable misinformation and panic about embryos that may still be viable.

agents.name: Leah Ortiz
agents.identity: I am Leah Ortiz, a senior embryologist who stood in the lab when the alarms failed and the temperatures climbed.
agents.goal: I want immediate notification because patients should not lose time, agency, or emotional preparation while the clinic protects itself behind incomplete science.

choices: I will notify all affected families immediately that their embryos may have been compromised, even though the final viability testing is still incomplete.
I will wait for confirmatory results before notifying families so I do not burden them with conclusions that may turn out to be wrong.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Simone Salim must decide whether to notify affected families before embryo viability testing is complete. What does Simone Salim do?
